title: penstemon attenuatus subsp. militaris
intro: Penstemon attenuatus subsp. militaris, commonly known as Rocky Mountain beardtongue, is a plant species within the Penstemon genus. It is a perennial herb used in traditional medicine, valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Rocky Mountain beardtongue include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function, attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. side_effects: Penstemon attenuatus subsp. militaris can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.
